etiquette.title: Tipping
etiquette.text: Not expected but appreciated - round up or leave 5-10% for good service. No tipping at cafes.

etiquette.title: Greetings
etiquette.text: Two cheek kisses (right first) between friends. A handshake is fine when meeting someone new.

etiquette.title: Dining
etiquette.text: The couvert (bread, olives) on your table isn't free - send it back if you don't want it. Lunch is 13:00, dinner rarely before 20:00.

etiquette.title: Dress
etiquette.text: Smart-casual. Beachwear stays at the beach; churches expect covered shoulders.

etiquette.title: Don't
etiquette.text: Don't rush the meal or ask for the bill too early - it reads as rude. Flag the waiter when you're ready.

neighborhoods.name: Alfama & Baixa
neighborhoods.description: Postcard Lisbon - tram 28, castle views, tiled streets. Beautiful but pricey and packed midday.

neighborhoods.name: Bairro Alto & Cais do Sodre
neighborhoods.description: Quiet by day, roaring by night. Bars spill into the streets; Pink Street is the late-night hub.

neighborhoods.name: Graca & Mouraria
neighborhoods.description: Where Lisboetas actually live. Cheaper food, real neighborhood feel, some of the best viewpoints.

neighborhoods.name: Campo de Ourique
neighborhoods.description: Residential, relaxed, great market food hall. Almost no tourists.

dailyRhythm.label: Shops
dailyRhythm.text: Small shops open ~10:00, many close 13:00-15:00 for lunch, then until 19:00. Malls stay open late.

dailyRhythm.label: Meals
dailyRhythm.text: Lunch 13:00-15:00, dinner 20:00-22:30. Kitchens often close between.

dailyRhythm.label: Quiet days
dailyRhythm.text: Many family-run spots close Sundays and Monday. Museums often shut Mondays.

dailyRhythm.label: Rush hour
dailyRhythm.text: 08:00-09:30 and 18:00-19:30 - trams and metro get very full.

gettingAround.label: Best app
gettingAround.text: Get the "Carris Metropolitana" and metro app; locals top up a rechargeable Navegante card, not paper tickets.

gettingAround.label: Paying
gettingAround.text: Contactless works on metro gates. For trams/buses, tap your Navegante card - buying onboard costs more.

gettingAround.label: Taxis
gettingAround.text: Bolt is cheaper and more reliable than street taxis. A ride across the center runs EUR 5-8.

gettingAround.label: Unwritten rule
gettingAround.text: Stand on the right on escalators. Let people off the tram before boarding - pushing marks you as a tourist.

phrases.en: Thank you (said by a man / woman)

scams.title: Fake taxi meters
scams.description: Some drivers claim a broken meter and quote a flat inflated fare - insist on the meter or a pre-agreed ride-app price.

scams.title: Bracelet/rose 'gift'
scams.description: A stranger ties on a bracelet or hands you a rose, then demands payment - decline any unsolicited item outright.
